raw_transcript_text:
DOCTOR: Namaste, kya haal hai aapke? Surgery ko ab do hafte ho gaye hain, right?

PATIENT: Ji, haan. Theek hoon, par thoda pain hota hai kabhi kabhi, aur walk karne mein bhi dikkat hoti hai.

DOCTOR: Theek hai, dekhte hain. Pehle check karte hain... BP toh check karte hain — aapka BP abhi kya hai? Ghar pe bhi check kiya tha?

PATIENT: Haan ji, kal subah check kiya tha — one forty over ninety tha, lekin aaj subah dawai nahi li thi.

DOCTOR: Achha, theek hai. BP medication — aapko Metolar 25 hai na? Subah aur raat? Ek baar aur confirm karte hain.

PATIENT: Haan, wahi hai. Lekin doctor sahab, chakkar bhi aate hain kabhi kabhi — specially subah uthne pe.

DOCTOR: Koi baat nahi. That could be due to BP fluctuations. Let's check your vitals. Aapki pulse rate bhi dekhte hain — aaj subah kya thi?

PATIENT: Subah nahi liya, lekin kal raat pulse meter pe ninety three tha.

DOCTOR: Theek hai. Temperature bhi check karte hain — haath toh garam nahi lag raha? Fever jaise kuch nahi hai?

PATIENT: Nahi, fever nahi hai. Bas wound ke aas-paas halka redness hai, par dard nahi.

DOCTOR: Achha, wound dikhao toh sahi. (pauses to examine) Hmm, mild erythema hai, but no discharge. Theek hai, infection ke signs nahi hain. Aapko antibiotics toh complete kiya tha na — Augmentin 625?

PATIENT: Haan, do din aur baaki hain. Abhi bhi le rahi hoon.

DOCTOR: Very good. Continue that. Apan ek course poori tarah se karna hai — nahi to infection ho sakta hai. Ab aap ka pain kis tarah ka hai? Thoda dull ache hai, ya sharp?

PATIENT: Halka dull ache hai — specially jab walk karti hoon. Lift uthane mein bhi takleef hoti hai.

DOCTOR: Normal hai. Aapne Sutures toh abhi bhi hain — woh 10 din mein remove kareinge. Tab tak bed rest and no heavy lifting. Chalo, aapko ek naya prescription likh deta hoon.

(Prescription block)

**Prescription for Post-surgical Follow-Up**
- **Augmentin 625** — 1 tab twice daily, for 5 more days
- **Dolo-650** — If needed for pain, max 2 tabs in 24 hours
- **Metolar 25** — 1 tab twice daily (continue as before)
- **Ecosprin 75** — 1 tab at night, for 1 month (thromboprophylaxis)
- **Shelcal 500** — 1 tab at bedtime, for 3 months
- **Telma-40** — Half tab in morning, for BP (if needed, based on BP readings)
- **Pan-D** — 1 tab before breakfast, for GERD prevention (if acidity occurs)

**Dosage Note:** Continue Pan-D for one week. If no acidity, stop.

**Advice:** Mild walking allowed; no stairs. Keep wound dry. Change dressing every 3 days. Report for suture removal on Day 10.

DOCTOR: Theek hai? Koi aur question?

PATIENT: Nahi sahab, aapne hi sab clear kar diya. Bas pain ki dawai kya raat mein le loon?

DOCTOR: Haan, raat mein Dolo-650 le sakti hain. Sone se pehle — is se pain bhi kam hoga aur neend bhi achhi aayegi.

PATIENT: Thank you, doctor sahab.

DOCTOR: Welcome, theek hai. Agli baar sutures ke time milte hain. Tab tak dhyaan rakho.

soap_note.subjective: Patient reports occasional dull ache, worse with walking and lifting. Difficulty walking. Dizziness upon waking. No fever. Mild redness around wound but no pain at wound site. BP was 140/90 yesterday morning, but had not taken medication that morning. Pulse 93 last night. Completed most of Augmentin 625 course.
soap_note.objective: Vitals: BP (not measured today), pulse 93 (last night), no fever. Wound examination: mild erythema, no discharge, sutures in place.
soap_note.assessment: Post-surgical follow-up, 2 weeks after surgery. Mild wound erythema without signs of infection. BP fluctuations possibly related to missed medication. Dizziness likely due to BP variability.
soap_note.plan: Continue Augmentin 625 1 tab twice daily for 5 more days. Use Dolo-650 as needed for pain, max 2 tabs/day, preferably at bedtime. Continue Metolar 25 1 tab twice daily. Start Ecosprin 75 1 tab at night for 1 month (thromboprophylaxis). Start Shelcal 500 1 tab at bedtime for 3 months. Start Telma-40 half tab in morning if needed based on BP readings. Start Pan-D 1 tab before breakfast for 1 week if acidity occurs. Advise mild walking, no stairs, keep wound dry, change dressing every 3 days, suture removal on day 10.
